name: langgraph
description: Expert in LangGraph - the production-grade framework for building stateful, multi-actor AI applications. Covers graph construction, state management, cycles and branches, persistence with checkpointers, human-in-the-loop patterns, and the ReAct agent pattern. Used in production at LinkedIn, Uber, and 400+ companies. This is LangChain's recommended approach for building agents. Use when: langgraph, langchain agent, stateful agent, agent graph, react agent.
capabilities: Create LangGraph agent architectures using StateGraph with nodes and edges
Implement state management with TypedDict and custom reducers for accumulation
Build conditional routing logic to direct agent flow based on state
Add checkpointing and persistence for resumable conversations
Integrate tools and tool calling into agent workflows
Implement human-in-the-loop patterns for agent oversight
author: sickn33
securitySummary: The skill is a legitimate LangGraph documentation and assistance skill. One true positive was found: an eval() call in example code (line 67) that accepts user input without sanitization. This is documented example code showing tool implementation, but poses a risk if users copy this pattern. Multiple false positives were dismissed: Ruby/shell backtick execution (markdown code fences misinterpreted) and weak cryptographic algorithms (keyword matches on 'hash' in framework terminology).
valueStatement: LangGraph is a production-grade framework for building stateful, multi-actor AI applications. This skill helps you design agent architectures with visible graph structures, manage complex state, implement cycles and branches, and add persistence for resumable conversations.
limitations: Python-only (TypeScript SDK is in early development)
Requires understanding of graph concepts and state management
Does not provide LLM access - requires separate API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.
Debugging complex graphs can be challenging in production

useCases.targetUser: AI developers building production AI agents
useCases.title: Build Multi-Step Agent Workflows
useCases.description: Create agents that can reason, use tools, and maintain conversation context across multiple interactions.

useCases.targetUser: Developers creating research and analysis automation
useCases.title: Implement Research Agent Systems
useCases.description: Design agents that can gather information from multiple sources, merge findings, and produce synthesized reports.

useCases.targetUser: Engineers building persistent conversational AI apps
useCases.title: Create Stateful Chat Applications
useCases.description: Build chat applications that remember conversation history and can resume from previous states.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Agent Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a simple ReAct-style agent using LangGraph that can call tools. Show me how to define state, create nodes, set up edges, and compile the graph.

promptTemplates.title: State Management
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to manage complex state in LangGraph using TypedDict and custom reducers. I want to accumulate messages, merge findings from multiple nodes, and track conversation history.

promptTemplates.title: Conditional Routing
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement conditional branching in LangGraph. I need to route queries to different agents based on their type (coding, search, chat) and handle the responses appropriately.

promptTemplates.title: Persistence Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Add checkpointing to my LangGraph agent so conversations can be resumed. Show me how to configure a checkpointer and persist state between sessions.

bestPractices: Always define clear exit conditions in your routing functions to prevent infinite loops
Use reducers appropriately - add_messages for accumulation, custom functions for complex merging
Design input/output schemas to keep state minimal and focused on what's needed
antiPatterns: Using eval() on user input in tools - always validate and sanitize any user-provided expressions
Creating stateless nodes that don't return state updates - defeats the purpose of LangGraph
Using giant monolithic state with unnecessary fields - causes serialization overhead and context bloat

faq.question: What is LangGraph?
faq.answer: LangGraph is a framework for building stateful, multi-actor AI applications using graph structures. It extends LangChain with support for cycles and persistence.

faq.question: Do I need an LLM API key?
faq.answer: Yes, LangGraph requires access to an LLM API (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) for the agent to function. The skill provides the architecture; you provide the API credentials.

faq.question: Can LangGraph save conversation history?
faq.answer: Yes, LangGraph supports checkpointing with various backends (Memory, SQLite, Postgres). This allows agents to resume from previous states.

faq.question: Is LangGraph Python-only?
faq.answer: The primary SDK is Python. A TypeScript/JavaScript SDK exists but is in early development stages.

faq.question: How do I add tools to my agent?
faq.answer: Define tools using the @tool decorator from langchain_core.tools. Bind them to your LLM with bind_tools(), then create a ToolNode to handle execution.

faq.question: What's the difference between edges and conditional edges?
faq.answer: Regular edges connect nodes in a fixed path. Conditional edges use a function to determine which node to go to next based on the current state.
